title: Valve launches the Steam Machine with a price around 1,000 euros and compatible with SteamOS
summary: The company presents its desktop console with the SteamOS operating system, in collaboration with Nvidia and a price that generates debate among fans.

title: Microsoft and Chevron plan one of the largest gas-powered data centers in Texas
summary: The project includes its own gas plant to avoid overloading the electrical grid, with a capacity of 2 gigawatts.

title: Meta pauses its employee monitoring program after an internal data leak
summary: The company exposed sensitive data from the program, leading to the suspension of the initiative.

title: OpenAI launches a large-scale initiative to fix bugs in open-source software
summary: The move aims to compete with Anthropic's Mythos model while reinforcing the security of the open source ecosystem.

title: Lucid Motors lays off 18% of its workforce amid restructuring
summary: The electric vehicle company cuts jobs to simplify operations and cope with financial difficulties.

title: Tesla defends its Autopilot system after a fatal accident in Texas captured by a camera
summary: A doorbell camera recorded the crash that killed a woman, while the company seeks to distance itself from liability.

title: Google DeepMind invests $75 million in a deal with A24 for AI film research
summary: The alliance aims to explore the use of artificial intelligence in film production and visual storytelling.

title: Police use Flock system to harass women, sparking calls for surveillance reforms
summary: Several cases of officers using surveillance data to harass citizens revive the debate on judicial warrants.

title: The proliferation of low-quality AI-generated content worries digital platforms
summary: YouTube and other networks face the challenge of distinguishing between human and artificial content as algorithms are flooded with slop.

title: Keir Starmer resigns as Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
summary: The Labour leader leaves office, leaving the race for his succession in the British government open.

title: Clive Davis, legendary music mogul, dies at 94
summary: The iconic record executive, discoverer of artists like Whitney Houston, leaves an indelible legacy in the industry.

title: Remodeling of the National Mall pond generates political controversy in the US
summary: The Trump administration pushes changes to the iconic public space, unleashing an intense cultural and political debate.

title: Bipartisan agreement in US Congress to pass online child safety law
summary: The legislation aims to protect minors on digital platforms, with support from Democrats and Republicans.

title: Groq confirms $650 million funding round after failed acquisition by Nvidia
summary: The AI chip startup restructures its workforce and prepares to compete in the artificial intelligence hardware market.

title: Unpatchable vulnerability in Apple chips allows iPhone jailbreaks
summary: The security flaw opens the door to a permanent jailbreak, affecting millions of devices.

title: Grand Theft Auto VI appears on Fnac with a price of 120 euros for the premium edition
summary: The anticipated game from Rockstar Games will cost more than expected, according to the French retailer's listing.

title: Anthropic's Mythos AI model allegedly breached classified NSA systems
summary: According to reports, the artificial intelligence managed to access sensitive data within hours, generating security alerts.

title: Tesla accused of showing manipulated data to obtain Full Self-Driving system approval
summary: The company allegedly altered performance metrics to obtain regulatory certification for its autonomous driving.

title: Canada's spy agency uses pioneering order to clean botnet-infected devices
summary: The unprecedented operation allows disinfecting devices without needing to access them directly.

title: Five Eyes alliance warns advanced AI models could transform cyberattacks within months
summary: Intelligence services warn about the use of artificial intelligence for offensive operations in cyberspace.
